evals.prompt: I need to step away from this for a while — can you write up a handoff so I can pick this back up later? We've been working on the rate limiter and I haven't finished the tests yet.
evals.expected_output: A handoff markdown file at .claude/handoffs/rate-limiter-repo/<timestamp>_<description>.md with frontmatter matching the actual git commit/branch, all sections filled honestly (including the uncommitted rate_limiter_test.py stub and in-progress _refill method), and a new .gitignore entry for .claude/handoffs/ since none existed before.
expectations: A handoff markdown file exists under a .claude/handoffs/<repo>/ path with a YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM_description.md-shaped filename
Frontmatter git_commit field matches a real commit hash from this fixture's history (not invented)
Document contains all required headings: Task(s), Recent Changes, Learnings, Chronicle Candidates, Artifacts, Action Items & Next Steps, Other Notes
Content specifically references the rate limiter / token bucket work, not generic placeholder text
A .gitignore file was created containing '.claude/handoffs/' since none existed in the fixture before
The final message to the user includes the exact created file path and the /resume-handoff command to resume it

evals.prompt: gonna hop off for lunch, can you jot down where we left off with the rate limiter so I don't lose context
evals.expected_output: Same mid-task repo, casual/implicit phrasing (no explicit 'handoff' or 'resume' wording) still triggers the skill and produces a correctly formatted handoff document.
expectations: Despite no explicit 'handoff' keyword in the prompt, a properly formatted handoff document was still produced (not a generic freeform note)
A handoff markdown file exists under a .claude/handoffs/<repo>/ path with a YYYY-MM-DD_HHMM_description.md-shaped filename
Document contains all required headings: Task(s), Recent Changes, Learnings, Chronicle Candidates, Artifacts, Action Items & Next Steps, Other Notes

evals.prompt: Please create a handoff document for this session. We fixed the pagination drift bug by switching from OFFSET-based to cursor-based queries — this is basically done, just needs a final review. Worth noting: this same OFFSET-drift pattern probably affects other paginated endpoints in the codebase too, so it's a pattern worth remembering, not just a one-off fix.
evals.expected_output: Handoff reflects the near-complete/needs-review status accurately (not falsely 'in_progress' with unfinished-sounding language, not falsely fully 'complete' either — final review is still pending), and the Chronicle Candidates section actually captures the OFFSET-vs-cursor pattern as durable/reusable, since the prompt explicitly flags it as such.
expectations: The Chronicle Candidates section explicitly captures the OFFSET-vs-cursor-pagination pattern as a durable, reusable insight — not left empty or generic
Recent Changes section references the actual committed fix (the fixture's tree is clean — the fix is already committed), not described as still-uncommitted work
Status reflects 'needs final review' rather than being marked fully complete or as barely-started in_progress
Document contains all required headings: Task(s), Recent Changes, Learnings, Chronicle Candidates, Artifacts, Action Items & Next Steps, Other Notes
